if tostring(0.0) == "0.0" then   -- "standard" coercion float->string
  assert('' .. 12 == '12' and 12.0 .. '' == '12.0')
  assert(tostring(-1203 + 0.0) == "-1203.0")
else   -- compatible coercion
  assert(tostring(0.0) == "0")
  assert('' .. 12 == '12' and 12.0 .. '' == '12')
  assert(tostring(-1203 + 0.0) == "-1203")
end

do  -- tests for '%p' format
  -- not much to test, as C does not specify what '%p' does.
  -- ("The value of the pointer is converted to a sequence of printing
  -- characters, in an implementation-defined manner.")
  local null = "(null)"    -- nulls are formatted by Lua
  assert(string.format("%p", 4) == null)
  assert(string.format("%p", true) == null)
  assert(string.format("%p", nil) == null)
  assert(string.format("%p", {}) ~= null)
  assert(string.format("%p", print) ~= null)
  assert(string.format("%p", coroutine.running()) ~= null)

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV

tool_use_result.stdout:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV

content.content:
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then

tool_use_result.stdout:

delta.text: Found the bug. In `vm.rs:997-1001`, when concatenating empty-string with a number (
`'' .. 12`), the code copies `top-1` to `top-2` without converting the number to a string first. The C source guarantees `
top-1` is already string-converted via `tostring(L, s2v(top - 1))` in the entry condition.

content.text: Found the bug. In `vm.rs:997-1001`, when concatenating empty-string with a number (`'' .. 12`), the code copies `top-1` to `top-2` without converting the number to a string first. The C source guarantees `top-1` is already string-converted via `tostring(L, s2v(top - 1))` in the entry condition.

lines: -            // C: setobjs2s(L, top-2, top-1); consumed 2 inputs → 1 result

content.text:
Build is green (no errors, only pre-existing warnings).

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:997-1001` (in `concat()`). The empty-string short-circuit `else if is_empty(&v_tm2)` copies `top-1` straight into `top-2` without first coercing it to a string. In the C source (`lvm.c`'s `luaV_concat`), `top-1` is already string-converted because `tostring(L, s2v(top - 1))` runs as part of the entry-condition's OR — so the C version never copies a raw number through. The Rust port only used a `matches!` type-check (no in-place conversion), so `'' .. 12` produced the number `12` instead of the string `"12"`, breaking `strings.lua` line 152 `assert('' .. 12 == '12' and 12.0 .. '' == '12.0')`.

**Fix:** Call `state.coerce_to_string(top - 1)?` inside the `is_empty(&v_tm2)` branch before cloning/copying, so numbers are stringified to match C semantics.

**New state:** Not re-tested per instructions (mega-loop will re-test). `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. The standalone repro `local x = '' .. 12; print(type(x))` previously printed `number`; with this fix it should now print `string`, allowing the assertion at line 152 to pass.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:997-1001` (in `concat()`). The empty-string short-circuit `else if is_empty(&v_tm2)` copies `top-1` straight into `top-2` without first coercing it to a string. In the C source (`lvm.c`'s `luaV_concat`), `top-1` is already string-converted because `tostring(L, s2v(top - 1))` runs as part of the entry-condition's OR — so the C version never copies a raw number through. The Rust port only used a `matches!` type-check (no in-place conversion), so `'' .. 12` produced the number `12` instead of the string `"12"`, breaking `strings.lua` line 152 `assert('' .. 12 == '12' and 12.0 .. '' == '12.0')`.

**Fix:** Call `state.coerce_to_string(top - 1)?` inside the `is_empty(&v_tm2)` branch before cloning/copying, so numbers are stringified to match C semantics.

**New state:** Not re-tested per instructions (mega-loop will re-test). `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. The standalone repro `local x = '' .. 12; print(type(x))` previously printed `number`; with this fix it should now print `string`, allowing the assertion at line 152 to pass.
